title: Zine cover design basics
sources: SYNTHESISED — zine cover design draws on the photocopier aesthetic tradition of 1970s-1990s punk and DIY zines, and on general print-design principles. No pre-1928 PD source.
notes: READING article on the design decisions for a self-published zine cover. Cover: what makes a zine cover work (legible title, clear genre signal, visual contrast on a photocopied page), common design approaches (hand-drawn title lettering, photocopy-collage, typewriter text, simple two-tone linocut), the title-to-image hierarchy, what to avoid (too much detail — photocopiers lose halftones), how the cover relates to the binding (saddle-staple vs accordion vs pamphlet all have slightly different cover constraints). Deliberately a practical article, not an art-theory lecture. Includes a worked example: three design options for a single hypothetical zine cover and what each communicates.
